Type: Purple Thoughtz is the debut EP of Philedelphia rapper Lil Uzi Vert. The album was self-released onto Soundcloud on January 19, 2014. Coming out of their former rap group "Steaktown" and off one of their most popular songs titled Steaktown Anthem, Purple Thoughtz would serve as an introduction to Uzi's solo work as an artist prior to the release of their first mixtape.

Name:
Luv Is Rage 2 [V1]
(LIR2 V1)
Notes:
(7/31/16) (The Perfect LUV Tape releases)
(1/??/17) (Uzi goes to Hawaii to rework the project)
Type:
The original version of Luv Is Rage 2, which was completed in 2016.  Because of this, the project was ready to release in late 2016 or very early 2017, but ended up getting delayed. The change was because of Uzi travelling to Hawaii in January of 2017 where he started to play with a darker theme for the album. This likely made him realize how it could be better to rework the album, since this 2016 version was way too similar to The Perfect LUV Tape in terms of sound so he scrapped it in favor of developing the new gritty and dark version from 2017. 
The cover for this era is made by @mattmeiners_ (The creator of the official Luv Is Rage 2 Cover Art)

The EP features production from Honorable C.N.O.T.E., D. Rich, Mannie Fresh, Zaytoven, DP Beats and GLOhan Beats. The project was planned to release fully on streaming, but didn't due to sample clearance issues and has stayed on Soundcloud since.

(LIR2 V2, Hawaii Sessions)
Notes:
(January ??, 2017) (Uzi goes to Hawaii to rework the project)
(February 26, 2017) (Luv Is Rage 1.5 'releases')
(August 25, 2017) (Luv Is Rage 2 is released)
(November 11, 2017) (Luv Is Rage 2 Deluxe is released)
Type: The debut studio album by the American rapper and singer Lil Uzi Vert, released on August 25, 2017, through Generation Now and Atlantic Records. The project contains heavy production from Don Cannon, Maaly Raw, Pi'erre Bourne and DP Beats. This album was highly anticipated since before Lil Uzi tweeted on December 13, 2016 regarding the release, but has been waited on since November of 2016. To much surprise, Uzi decided to leave out one of the songs that was most highly anticipated “Come This Way”, which was first previewed on his Instagram account, and performed in concerts, according to the producer, LyleLeDuff. LeDuff stated that he told him the fans wanted it but Uzi said he didn’t care. The release date was finally announced on August 25th, where Uzi tweeted out some leaks of snippets and his cover art/tracklist.

Name:
Lil Uzi Vert vs. The World 2 [V1]
(LUV vs. The World 2, Luv Is Rage 2 (Deluxe))
Notes:
(August 25, 2017) (Luv Is Rage 2 is released)
(November 17, 2017) (Luv Is Rage 2 (Deluxe) is released, does not contain any tracks recorded from this era)
(December 12-15, 2017) (An idea folder of songs intended for VTW2 is put together, Uzi would shortly after go on hiatus)
(December 26, 2017) (Uzi resumes making music, with newer songs featuring a new tone in his voice)
(January ??, 2018) (Eternal Atake is conceptualized)
Type:
Uzi’s planned sequel to Lil Uzi Vert vs. The World was announced on September 15, 2017, with a preview of “Zoom.” After Luv Is Rage 2, he had no album concept and was recording freely; none of these songs were meant for the LIR2 deluxe. This era blended LIR2’s melodies with early Eternal Atake ideas and marked the beginning of Uzi’s long-running collaboration with producers from the infamous Working on Dying group (F1LTHY, Oogie Mane, and Forza), alongside many other producers like Ike Beatz, Bugz Ronin, Don Cannon, DP Beats and Wheezy. Uzi would also start beefing with Maaly Raw, resulting in him not sending any beats to Uzi until 2020-2021. After a short December 2017 break, Uzi returned with a new vocal tone—suggesting he shelved LUVvsTW2 to focus on EA. The concept was later revived as EA’s deluxe, combining fan‑requested songs from multiple eras with previously unheard tracks.
(The cover art is originally meant for "ROLLI" made by @fqrris. This cover art is reasoned to be the closest ideal of the project's original cover.)

Name:
16*29
(Collaboration with Playboi Carti)
Notes:
(Mid 2017) (The project is concieved)
(September 29, 2017) (Carti infamously tweets "Uzi Carti * Carti Uzi * Tape ... loading")
(January 3-4, 2018) (Major sessions for the project occur)
(September 25, 2018) (Chinatown releases Squad from 2016 on SoundCloud before it gets taken down, project is likely scrapped around this time)
Type:
In September of 2017, Playboi Carti announced via Snapchat and Twitter that he would be collaborating with Lil Uzi Vert on a joint album titled 1629*. The following month, a tour for the album was announced by the two, but it was shortly canceled afterward when Uzi said he would not be going on tour with Carti because he "needed to focus." The album would fall by the wayside until July of 2018, when Carti mentioned that he and Uzi had recorded "like 100 songs." After that, all talk of the album pretty much subsided. It should be noted that many insiders have claimed that there was never a tracklist, cover art, or marketing plan put together.
The cover for this era is fanmade.

Type: On July 16, 2018, Uzi would announce his next album on his IG titled "Eternal Atake" - which means "Forever Overtake". The record was already conceptualized at the start of the year and Kesha Lee teased the concept back in February. He introduced a new theme for this project, which is visually (cover art) and thematically (songs) influenced by the logo of the Heaven’s Gate cult that infamously committed a mass suicide in 1997. Obviously, he received backlash and was threatened to be sued over the artwork for this creative decision. This era is a direct continuation from all work and ideas done during LUVvsTW 2 [V1]. Uzi hopped on more spacey and catchy beats during this era from producers such as Harper, Bugz Ronin, Forza, Oogie Mane, and others.

Name:
Eternal Atake [V2]
(ETERNAL ATAKE, eternal. [ ih-tur-nl ], Tranquility)
Notes:
(March 24, 2019) (Uzi fumbles his set release date and delays the album due to label issues)
(March 25, 2019) (Uzi signs a new deal with JAY-Z's Roc Nation and claims that he is reworking the album) 
(March 28, 2019) ("Free Uzi" is 'released' as an act of protest by Uzi against his label)
(April 9, 2019) ("Sanguine Paradise" and "That's A Rack" release as promotional singles)
(December 12, 2019) ("Futsal Shuffle 2020" releases, initiating Uzi's final rollout for the album)
Type: After label conflicts with Generation Now, Uzi delayed the album and sparked fan backlash. After DJ Drama claimed there was problems, Uzi fired back with a cryptic comment hinting at their dispute and launched the “FREE UZI” idea. He soon aligned with Roc Nation, giving him a way to keep recording while dealing with legal issues involving DJ Drama, Don Cannon, and GN. This marked the start of EA’s rework, previewed by the song “Free Uzi,” which shifted toward a trap‑focused sound and it's unique high pitched vocals in order to avoid copyright from Generation Now. Uzi continued developing East Coast–influenced flows and drew inspiration from New York styles while spending time with A Boogie wit da Hoodie. This era features production from Supah Mario, Brandon Finessin, Bugz Ronin, Oogie Mane and more. Ongoing label problems escalated when producer Forza was fired for leaking songs.

Name:
Eternal Atake [V3]
(Eternal Atake (Deluxe) - LUV vs. The World 2, Lil Uzi Vert vs. The World 2 [V2], "Uzi, Orenji, Baby Pluto")
Notes:
(December 12, 2019) ("Futsal Shuffle 2020" releases as a single)
(March 6, 2020) (Eternal Atake is released)
(March 13, 2020) (Eternal Atake (Deluxe) - LUV vs. The World 2 is released)
Type: The final rollout for the album. Considered to start off with the single release of “Futsal Shuffle 2020” on December 12, 2019, marking a return to the spacey, futuristic themes of V1 blended with his newer 2019 trap flows alongside new production from Clibbo and returning producers. Throughout early January 2020, he teased Eternal Atake with promotional images, tweets and revealing more information. Uzi would confirm on February 1, 2020 that the album would feature 16 tracks. Uzi unexpectedly dropped the full album on March 6 (a week earlier than expected). A fan theorized that it's eighteen tracks aligned with his three alter egos—a theory Uzi confirmed—he immediately began teasing a deluxe edition, which released on March 13, 2020, featuring long‑sought songs from the LIR2 and LUVvsTW 2 (V1) eras along with newly updated tracks.

(March 13, 2020) (Eternal Atake (Deluxe) - LUV vs. The World 2 is released)
(October 23, 2020) (Forever Young is conceived)
Type: After Eternal Atake, Uzi faced backlash over his flows and track choices, pushing him toward a new sound built around sparkly beats mostly crafted with longtime friend Brandon Finessin. This time is often seen as the start of a decline in quality as he prioritized output and experimentation alongside hiring Benjamin Thomas, whom many blamed for Uzi's vocals sounding poorly mixed in the following years as his lead engineer after Kesha Lee quit to pursue her own music career. In April, Uzi began working with Working On Dying and Brandon on high‑pitched “baby voice” tracks, a style Playboi Carti was also using while they collaborated closely, dropping back‑to‑back singles (@ MEH and Sasuke) and staging a fake beef as part of Carti’s rollout; both were criticized for the unintelligible flow, leading Carti to rework Whole Lotta Red and Uzi to keep searching for a new sound. Because of this, Uzi started working on Pluto x Baby Pluto. This era is essentially a gap of experimental songs with a new sound from Uzi without a general project in mind.

(May ??, 2020) (Future & Uzi start working on their collaborative project)

(November 17, 2020) (Pluto x Baby Pluto (Deluxe) is released)
Type: A collaborative project between Future and Uzi, which title comes from both of their alter egos; with Uzi tributing Future by once again using his "Baby Pluto" persona, that was previously introduced in Eternal Atake. The project features production from some more producers such as DJ ESCO, D. Hill, London on da Track and more.  After Uzi recorded solo songs with no paticular direction in early 2020, they would release two singles together, "Over Your Head" and "Patek", which coincidentally was on Uzi's birthday. They would later release the trailer for the album (which used the scrapped intro, "Astronauts"), revealing the release date and so on from there. The album was released unexpectedly to some, with somewhat of a positive reception. Uzi would tease the deluxe, which ended up dropping just a week later.

Name:
Forever Young
(Luv Is Rage 3 [V1], The Real Uzi 2, SoundCloud Tape [V1])
Notes:
(October 23, 2020) (Forever Young gets officially conceptualized, when Uzi responded to a comment on IG asking him to go back to his 2016-2017 flows) 
(November 17, 2020) (Pluto x Baby Pluto (Deluxe) is released, Uzi teases 'Forever Young' afterwards for the first time)
(December 17-23, 2020) (Uzi has many legendary IG Lives showcasing new tracks; confirming on the 20th of December that 3 new projects were on the way)
(February 17, 2021) (Uzi allegedly has a listening session for the album with Roc Nation)
(March 21, 2021) (Uzi makes a Tweet alluding to him also working on The Real Uzi 2)
(July 4, 2021) (Uzi gets in in a physical altercation with his label mate SAINt JHN and his ex-girlfriend)
(July 16, 2021) (Uzi announces "The Pink Tape" for the first time; Forever Young is still being worked on at that time)
(March ??, 2022) (Forever Young is officially scrapped)
Type:
Uzi responded to an IG comment asking him to return to his 2016–17 style by saying he would start that night, marking the beginning for Forever Young The concept had accelerated as he dyed his hair purple again and hosted multiple Instagram livestreams from December 17–23, previewing tons of new songs and snippets alongside announcing three projects planned for 2021: a SoundCloud mixtape, Forever Young, and Luv Is Rage 3, with new producers again such as Ty Luminocity and Pro Logic helping make a return to his old sound. Around this time he went viral for implanting a $24 million pink diamond in his forehead and for publicly confirming his relationship with JT. Uzi later tweeted The Real Uzi 2, confirming he was also working on a sequel to his 2014 tape. He officially announced The Pink Tape july but hadn't stopped working on Forever Young, as it remained active until it was finally scrapped in March 2022. The SoundCloud tape survived, and eventually released as Red & White in 2022, while Luv Is Rage 3 stayed in the idea stage until revisited in 2023. The Real Uzi 2 was notably worked on in the second half of this era and into Pink Tape.
The art for this era is was done by jleovisions on TikTok, who was commissioned by Uzi.

Name: 💿 The Real Uzi 2 [Mixtape]
Notes: Ever since Uzi Tweeted the sequel for his first mixtape (on March 21, 2021), many assumed that Uzi had moved on from Forever Young and dedicated himself to this project. Even though Uzi himself would never ever mention this project again, there are sources who claim that the tape consisted of many trap-influenced tracks, as well as other tracks that ended up in consideration for the Pink Tape. We know that this is true, due to the fact that Uzi did many trap songs during April and May of that year. There are others however, who claim that this project never actually made it past a concept, making its existence quite debatable.

Name:
Pink Tape [V1]
(Delta Pink, The Pink Tape, Forever Young [V2], SoundCloud Tape [V2], Astrokat)
Notes:
(July 16, 2021) (Uzi announces "The Pink Tape" for the first time)
(October ??, 2021) (The project slightly shifts to the Delta Pink concept, alongside the 'Astro Kat' persona)
(October 29, 2021) ("Demon High" releases as a single)
(December ??-17, 2021) (Delta Pink as a concept seemingly gets scrapped)
Type: Uzi announced Pink Tape in July 2021, he shifted toward a melodic, experimental, and rage‑rap sound with huge production from Brandon Finessin alongside others such as Bugz Ronin and ShaunGoBrazy. Uzi previewed new songs at Rolling Loud Miami and briefly teased Forever Young for the last time. Inspired partly by Playboi Carti and their brief reunion, he developed the project into Delta Pink, performing tracks like “Rage” and “5, 4, 3, 2, 1,” making major public appearances, and releasing “Demon High” as its lead single. During this period he dealt with legal issues from a an incident in July, adopted new looks, explored Jersey‑club influences, appeared on Adin Ross’ streams, and introduced the “Astro Kat” persona. But by late December he abandoned the Delta Pink concept, shifted toward a more aggressive sound, went quiet on social media, and entered a key phase of court‑mandated rehabilitation—ultimately scrapping the first phase of the project..

✨ Metro Boomin - Whitney Always [V1]
(with Lil Uzi Vert) (prod. Metro Boomin)
(At The Gates, Forgot I Was Famous, All Painted)
[Unfinished]
Notes:
OG Filename: Metro - Whitney Always - Ruff v1 2
First recorded version of "At The Gates" for Uzi, which doesn't contain Drake's vocals yet. Samples "I Will Always Love You" by Whitney Houston. Doesn't feature any unheard takes from already surfaced song, like it was previously believed. Was seemingly left titled after its instrumental at first.

Name:
⭐ Drake - Forgot I Was Famous [V5]
(with Lil Uzi Vert & Metro Boomin) (prod. Metro Boomin, SAUCEboy, Preme & 40)
(Whitney Always, At The Gates, All Painted)
Notes:
OG Filename: 13 Forgot I Was Famous 40 Mix 4 SEQ - master
Further version which was given to Drake. Was confirmed to have been track #13 of an early draft of "Certified Lover Boy" and was meant to transition info "Knife Talk", but was removed by request of Lil Yachty due to his beef with Uzi. Yachty had later tried to say that the sample ("I Will Always Love You" by Whitney Houston), wasn't cleared, and that it was the actual reason for it not dropping. It is not known which is the actual reason. The song was first previewed on September 6, 2021, by a DJ in a bowling alley, then on April 7, 2023 it was played on Drake's OVO Sirius XM/Sound 42 Radio, but with distortion on parts of the song. On Sep 29, 2022, the track's title at the time was confirmed through an IG story post by the photographer, Noah Dillon. Which captured a screen with the text "Drake - At The Gates ft. Lil Uzi", assigning the fan name as an official one. Full uncut version finally leaked on Oct 31, 2025.

Name: 💿 Delta Pink Tape
Notes: Adin Ross claimed that Uzi was finishing the album, on the night of December 2nd, 2021. We even have a tracklist that backs up the existence of at least one late 2021 copy, of what was at the time 'Delta Pink Tape'. There's a big chance that it was scheduled for a December 2021 release. But we also know that by December 17th, 2021, Uzi had scrapped the sparkly punk-rock idea Delta was going for, once he got asked by a fan to include "Party With The Demons" on the tape, but Uzi stated that he was now "on some street shit". He also started ti seemingly rework it by New Year's Eve, since he claimed that he was working alone on the studio during that night.

Name:
Super geëky
(Collaboration with Yeat)
(1600TR, Pink Lyfë, P!nk Lyfe, 1600twizz)
Notes:
(February ??, 2022) (Yeat and Uzi link up and start conceiving their joint project)
(??? ??, 2023) (The project gets scrapped once both artists start to lock in on their respective albums)
Type:
On December 12th, 2019, Yeat tweeted "Me n uzi gone have a collab tape". Yeat posted with Uzi to his Instagram on March 17, 2022 with the caption: "16TR #p!nklyfe…. ###ë## #reallygeekedoutmybodykid #geeky????", hinting that a collaborative tape between the artists could be on the way. BNYX® would confirm on March 25, 2022 that the two were working together for a project. On April 15, 2022, Yeat teased the project in a since-deleted tweet and IG story post, hinting that a collaborative project with Uzi was on the way: "Super geeky tha tape… loading #kollab". Throughout 2021 and 2022, Uzi and Yeat worked together very often, with even Uzi saying that they and Yeat had "100 songs" together, some of which are released and some which are expected to be on a collaboration project teased various times. Although the title is not confirmed, 'Super geeky' is the fan name for the Uzi x Yeat sessions as it was a listed as a name the last time. Not much has been said since 2022 for the project, and it is likely scrapped. 
The cover for this era is not official

Name:
Pink Tape [V2]
(RED & WHITE, The Melodic Blue)
Notes:
(December ??-17, 2021) (Delta Pink as a concept seemingly gets scrapped)
(February 2, 2022) (Uzi gets sentenced to 7 months of rehabilitation)
(April 1-12, 2022) (Uzi officially returns to the public eye)
(July 22-26, 2022) (RED & WHITE releases as an EP, having some updates with new songs throughout the days)
(August 31, 2022) (Uzi's rehab comes to an end)
(October 17, 2022) ("Just Wanna Rock" releases as a single after going viral on TikTok)
(December ??, 2022) (A version of the album was ready to be released)
(January ??, 2023) (The album takes a shift in its creative direction, switching to a darker and more experimental rock focus)
Type:
After the disaster for 2021's version of the project, Uzi decided to rework the album's sound and direction again with more unique sounding tracks. This time, he was influenced by the Philadelphia rap style; a more street influenced rap. During this time Uzi decided to finally drop a SoundCloud tape, and released the RED & WHITE EP.  The reception for this EP was pretty mixed; people weren't so excited by Uzi's street style, but did appreciate his more spacey-experimental flow alongside the release of classic snippets. This made him rework the album once again, playing with more aesthetics and ideas. After Uzi failed to properly release BELIEVE ME on the RED & WHITE EP, Uzi was put into a 'timeout' by his own label for not properly releasing music, derailing immediate plans for a proper release for the Pink Tape in 2022.
The cover art for this era is FANMADE

He only re-recorded the first verse, as part of his promotional collaboration with the brand. The rest of the track was recorded at Paramount Studios. There's a big chance that it was considered for the album, or that it was a promotional single for the brand (if the latter is true, it might've ended up getting scrapped due to the controversy that the brand got into). Name: 💿 Pink Tape [2022]
Notes: It's assumed that the album was intended to release by December of 2022. Since Uzi had one of his biggest singles of his career popping at that time, and another amount of hype after releasing RED & WHITE. By the information and tracks we have, we know that he was aiming for a Philly trap influenced album, mixing it with his newly introduced Jersey-Club theme. He actually kept teasing an imminent release when he was hanging around with Kai Cenat and his AMP crew. We also know that the album was definetly reworked by the early stages of January of 2023, due to an abrupt change in both the chosen beats, as well as the thematic of the album overall - shifting towards a demonic theme, and more experimental ideas and collaborations.

Name:
METROOOO PINK
(Collaboration with Metro Boomin)
(Metro Pink, Lil Uzi Vert vs. Metro Boomin, Lil Metro Boomin)
Notes:
(Mid-January, 2023) (Uzi and Metro Boomin start working on a collab tape while Pink Tape is still in the works)
(June 2, 2023) (Metro Boomin releases 'METRO BOOMIN PRESENTS SPIDER-MAN: ACROSS THE SPIDER-VERSE (SOUNDTRACK FROM AND INSPIRED BY THE MOTION PICTURE)', with two tracks featuring Uzi)
(Mid-Late 2023) (The project gets scrapped at some point in late 2023 due to both artists being busy with different projects)
Type:
In January of 2023, Metro and Uzi got back together in the studio after a long period of working seperately. The first confirmation of this tape's existence was a DM exchange from Uzi to Metro, asking for him to "send the tape". It was stated by someone in Uzi hub (aka. Grube), that the project was titled "METROOOO PINK", and was intended to relase sometime early in 2023; with it being scheduled to come out before the Pink Tape did. Ultimately, the tape got scrapped due to both artists being compromised with their repsective albums; Metro himself started working the soundtrack for the SPIDER-MAN: ACROSS THE SPIDER-VERSE movie, as well as two joint albums with Future. While Uzi dedicated himself to continue his sessions for the Pink Tape. @vaultofuzi would end up playing eight songs off the Tape on December 13, 2024, making the project publically known.
The cover for this era is not actually intended for this project; comes from the promotional art for the SPIDER-MAN movie.

However, since 2024 an infamous insider of Uzi's unreleased discography (@vaultofuzi) started to post snippets of many previously unheard tracks produced by Metro Boomin, claiming that they were part of a scrapped mixtape, that was supposed to release a few months before the Pink Tape. Notes:

Type: The final version of the album was built together starting in the beginning of 2023, after Uzi decided to become more experimental for the project. This version of the project sees more rage and hard hitting production from Akachi, Only1Shredder, Bugz Ronin and more such as the Snow Strippers for remixing. Uzi would tease several more songs through IG live & live performances in 2023. Despite Uzi entering rehab in 2022, Uzi would be seen smoking cigarettes frequently (highlighted during the final rollout), and he would still continue to do promos for Telegram/Instagram marijuana 'businesses', and he continued on with this into his rock-type style. Several 2023 iterations of the project were made as Uzi began to finalize the tracklist and album into the "experimental" album that would release.

Notes:
A grail since its first snippet surfaced. It was initially believed by many to be an AI/Fake track. The real file has only 1:20 minutes of vocals, and an open verse. This file has the vocals looped over the open verse instead. Which means that this is  technically a partial leak, since it was modified from it's original state. The sessions for this song are in the hands of leakers who claim that the song was never finished. 

For some time, there was a fake filename that claimed that this track was made on May of 2022. Which was completely illogical, since both artists had a way different flow back then. This got supported by @uzirareupdates revealing the real recording dates; with Carti recording his only part (the chorus) on May of 2023 (alongside 'FOMDJ'), and Uzi one day before the Pink Tape got released. So there's a chance that this track was another scrapped feature from Carti for Uzi's album, that didn't obviously make the cut.

It is being rumoured that there is no official bouce for this song, and that people owning the session put together the song with what was available in it - this is not confirmed

As a matter of fact, it was recently re-discovered that Brandon actually recorded the first demo for this track, based off of Uzi's lyrics from a voice memo (so this was never really Brandon's song, but rather a reference track for Uzi, based on his idea). Brandon shared a snippet of his ref. in May of 2023. Uzi would then record his entire take over Brandon's reference demo and kept his adlibs throughout the track. 

It was stated in January of 2026 that a version (other than the one that Uzi previewed) was available for $8k - It was then stated it was PB'd a few hours later (possibly Brandons demo but unknown). It's been stated Uzi's version of the song is not actually owned, since it was engineered by Ike instead of Ben or Spencer.

Name:
Barter 16
(B16🩸, Barter 16: The Mixtape)
Notes:
(June 30, 2023) (The Pink Tape is finally released)
(August 19, 2023) (The mixtape is partially leaked in response to Uzi lying one too many times about the release date)
(September 15-17, 2023) (Barter 16 misses its September 17 deadline even after being turned in, and is later scrapped)
(September 20, 2023) (Barter 16 gets removed from Uzi's IG profile description)
Type:
A project that paid homage to the (at the time) incarcerated Young Thug’s game-changing debut mixtape, "Barter 6", and is a play on words, regarding Uzi’s home street block of 1600. Uzi uses a style directly inspired by Young Thug while recording over beats from similar producers, just with the current timeframe's style. Throughout the project, Uzi made many statments claiming he would drop the album, such as when he would drop the album 'on Destroy Lonely's life' or when Lil 1 DTE reached 100K followers on Instagram. Many tracklists would then surface online from whiteboards spotted during IG lives. After Uzi lied one too many times, the leaking group MonkeyMafia leaks part of the album. Fans did not seem to appreciate the style Uzi was going for after hearing these leaks, resulting in the project being completely scrapped.
(Engineer iamBNJMIIN, would be replaced by Key and Trilla around August 2023).

Name:
Playboi Carti - JUSTIN BIEBER [V2]
(with Lil Uzi Vert) (prod. Maaly Raw, Ambezza & OUTOFAIR)
(VETEMENTS Jeans, By Myself)
Notes: After Vetements Jeans was scrapped from Pink Tape, it was considered and updated for I AM MUSIC. First previewed in a short snippet played by DJ Swamp Izzo on @lilbankhead’s Instagram Live — in honor of Playboi Carti’s 28th birthday. An attempt was made to preview the track on the V-103 radio show on September 13, 2023, however, due to Playboi Carti hanging up on Izzo, they had to delay the event until the following day. Carti “switched up” and prevented the station from playing the song on September 14. The only thing that is known about this version is that it features a DJ Swamp Izzo intro, it was supposed to be played on radio on Sept 14, 2023 and it has been considered for I AM MUSIC, before being scrapped from the project.

Name: 💿 Barter 16 [Mixtape]
Notes: The tape got officially turned in to DSPs on September 1st, 2023. That's according to London on da Track, who was the mixtape's executive producer. Just a couple hours prior to that, MIKE DEAN himself made a post claiming that he was the one who mixed it. However, due to an unfair early public reception, B16 was scrapped even before it's deadline. With many calling it unoriginal and effortless, only judging by the partially leaked copy that was both degraded and tagged by those responsible of its surfacing.

Name:
Luv Is Rage 3 [V2]
(LIR3, 💔3)
Notes:
(July 8, 2023) (Uzi teases Luv Is Rage 3 to drop in "a couple months")
(September 15-17, 2023) (Barter 16 misses its September 17 deadline, and is later scrapped)
(September 29, 2023) ("NFL" is released as a single, Uzi teases Luv Is Rage 3)
(December 25, 2023) (Uzi drops "Red Moon" as the lead single for the album)
(February 1, 2024) (Uzi changes his IG description to "LIR3 L o a d i n g 💔"; the album is being mixed at this point)
(February ??, 2024) (Luv Is Rage 3 is scrapped; Uzi moves on to a different project)
Type: Uzi announced Luv Is Rage 3 will be his final album before retirement. Not much is currently known about the project, aside from Uzi saying it's sound was closer to the first two Luv Is Rage projects. Uzi later said that he wasn't retiring, basically confirmomg the idea of LIR3 to be cancelled/scrapped for his next album alongside EA2 being announcemed. Funnily enough, thanks to Uzi's ex-assistant's testimony from a lawsuit, she confirmed that Uzi started to work on a new project in February of 2024. This can only mean that it was until then when this iteration of Luv Is Rage 3 got scrapped.

Name: 💿 Luv Is Rage 3
Notes:
Similarly to Forever Young, this project was ready to go by February of 2024. We even know that it was mixed and mastered, thanks to a post being made by the studio were the processes took place (February 1-8, 2024). We also learned that it was supposed to be an expedited release, that had a mix of old (probably fan-wanted) and new tracks.

Ever since Uzi and JAY-Z met up publicly (February 3rd and 4rth.), Uzi would never mention the album or its status again. In fact, he woud go completely silent from social media from then on. Apparently, Uzi didn't like the idea of an accelerated working process. We would later know (thanks to a statement done by his ex-assistant in a lawsuit against him) that he started working on "his new project" by February of 2024.

Name:
Eternal Atake 2
(EA 2, EA2)
Notes:
(February 2024) (Luv Is Rage 3 is scrapped; Uzi moves on to a different project)
(April 2024) (EA2 is conceived. Uzi begins to work on the album's concept)
(June-July 2024) (Major sessions for the album take place, involving Uzi's 'nitrous' sound and real addiction)
(August 2, 2024) (Uzi states that he is working on Eternal Atake 2 in an interview)
(November 1, 2024) (Eternal Atake 2 is released)
Type: Uzi first announced Eternal Atake 2 in an interview with someone who was asking what music the fans would hear next.  His response was 'Eternal Atake 2', leading fans to believe a new successor to the 2020 album is on the way. Cover posted by Uzi on his IG. Based on public speculation and past Uzi posts, fans believed that this album was forced by the label and Uzi didn't want to release it; however, other insiders have noted that Uzi thought this was going to be a "masterpiece". Upon the project's release, however, it quickly became apparent this was easily his worst project yet, with only a few songs even qualifying as half-decent.

With many still complaining about Trilla's bad mixing on songs from this time, and some even claiming the songs to be "unoriginal", this made Uzi comment that he was "testing the waters" after quickly releasing two songs exclusively on YouTube. Uzi would once again go silent until he returned to the public eye, where he would completely scrap the "ALL WHITE" idea, alongside teasing a new "Summer Tape", and wearing different styles.

Name:
W.H.2.U
(What Happened 2 Uzi, Summer Tape, The Perfect LUV Tape 2, Lil Uzi Vert vs. The World 3)
Notes:
(May 5, 2025) (Uzi returns and teases a 'Summer Tape', the concept of ALL WHITE is most likely scrapped at this point)
(June 20, 2025) (Uzi announces at the Fanatics Festival that the album is coming "This Summer")
(July 12, 2025) (Uzi teases a possible name of the project for the first time: "W.H.2.U🤷🏽‍♂️ Love yall forever ♾️")
(August 7, 2025) (Uzi previews "Hansen Helly", teasing the direction of the upcoming project)
(August ??, 2025) (Uzi scraps W.H.2.U. due to label issues and starts focusing on building his own imprint)
Type:
When asked about when was Uzi's new project releaseing, Don Cannon stated in an IG post that  "It'll drop when it drops 😎". The original question was actually: "Can you confirm Uzi is dropping The Perfect LUV Tape 2 this summer like people said", and it was made on May 2, 2025. It's unknown if this was an actual project in the works, a placeholder name, or Don Cannon evading the question. Uzi would later start teasing 'W.H.2.U', and the fans started theorizing about it's meaning; some even pointed out that this was actually teased on the promotional material from Eternal Atake 2. He made some IG stories, teasing this project as a "Summer Tape", and some people even speculated the possibility of "Lil Uzi Vert vs. The World 3" being also in the works. @purple4matter on X (an insider of the music industry) claimed that Uzi was set to drop this project on August. But after he started sharing many snippets (pressumably as part of the rollout), he allegedly got into a conflict with his label, which made him either scrap it or delay it indefinetly. He then shifted his focus into working on building his own record label (XVI Records). 
The cover used for this era is FANMADE.

He's been allegedly looking for artists to sign, and this could be confirmed by many recent snippets from underground artists, that go Uzi featured in them. So there's also a chance that he's planning on making a tape showcasing this signees, or other events to promote his new label. Because of this, there's a chance that he's shifted to setting his fifth studio album as a low priority. But only time will tell.

Notes: Harper confirmed in Uzi Hub that he did respond to MeanXVI (Uzi's manager) call, and that they are now once again working together.

Name: [Unknown Playboi Carti (& OPIUM?) Sessions]
Notes: Many people were speculating and getting hyped off the idea that Uzi would be a guest at Playboi Carti's 'ANTAGONIST' show, in Philly (November 10, 2025). However, this did not happen. Meek Mill turned out to be the special guest, despite Uzi's team being present at the venue. And yet, Carti did shoutout Uzi ("16*29 FOREVER"). He even stated: "I just saw my best friend Uzi", while also shouting out Maaly Raw. Even though there's a chance the two have finally got back to work together, this can also be very unlikely.

Name: [Unknown prettifun Sessions]
Notes: A picture surfaced of prettifun and Uzi working in the studio. prettifun would later repost it.

Name: [Unknown ZeeGoinXrazy Sessions]
Notes: On October 18, Zee shared that he was working consistently with Uzi and sending him tons of beats. He said that they were going for a nostalgic direction, with beats that ressembled "Dasani", "Poppin' Now" and many more. Using many video game and pop culture references as samples. It seems like Zee has now taken over as Uzi's main producer, and will be an key for this era's development. He also stated that they've been working for a long time now; at least all of 2025.

Notes: GUIDE & INFORMATION

Name: What does each emoji mean?
Notes: What is written below each song title?
Date of Recording: What do the colors on the fourth line* represent?
Availability: What do the colors on the third line* represent?

Name: (🏆): Grails = These are unreleased songs that the community generally wants the most on the tracker.
Notes:
Dance -- this first line shows the title of the song.
(prod. Bugz Ronin) -- this second line shows who produced the song, and who is featured on the song (if applicable)
(DM, Bet I'll Survive) -- this third line* shows extra names for the song, such as snippet names, instrumental names etc.
[Unfinished] -- this fourth line shows extra 'sub tags' for the song to describe 'features' of the song. This line will always be colored to differentiate it from the third line.




*Third line (mainly for other song names), sometimes isn't a thing as they only have one name, so the fourth line would be in the third line instead for these certain songs.
Date of Recording:
[Deep Voice] -- If a track heavily uses the infamous low-pitch voice effect, denominated by the fans as "Deep Voice". (some songs use deep voice but aren't notable enough (ie. background vocals/1line etc.) so it won't be tagged with it)

[Unfinished] -- If a track is unfinished, i.e.: it has an open verse without vocals recorded, misses a feature and has an open verse, etc.

[Rock Style] -- If a track is directly a rock song, is inspired or samples the genre.

[Jersey Club] -- If a track is directly a Jersey Club song, is inspired or samples the genre.
Availability:
(Officially verified name) -- Either the track got released on DSPs under that name, or it's an alternative name from a released song that was used at some point.

(Verified name) -- A verified name (or alternative name) for an unreleased track that was verified by filename(s), tracklists or any other formal data. It could also be that it was confirmed by a producer, or a member from Uzi's team. This would also be applied for alternative names for released songs that were never properly registered with the specifications mentioned above.

(Possible name) -- Speculated names for tracks that were likely stated by unreliable/not-fully-verified sources, or pure speculation.

(Fan name) -- Any and all names given by fans and insiders that are not official, nor confirmed by any reliable source (snippet names).

(Instrumental name) -- The name of the instrumental used for the song. Frequently used for unnamed bounces, and many tracks that were left titled after their instrumental.

Name: (🥉): Wanted = These are notable songs that people want, but not necessarily grails
(✨): Specials = These are songs that are still notable, but aren't considered to be a best of one.
(⭐): Best Of = These are songs that the fans generally consider to be the best music on the tracker.
